revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 皆神神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=長野市}} |所在地= [[長野県]][[長野市]]松代町豊栄5454 |祭神= [[出速雄命]]
[[伊邪那岐尊]]
[[伊邪那美尊]]
[[速玉男命]]
[[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建= [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年） |本殿= |別名= 熊野出速雄神社 |例祭= [[10月10日]] |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''皆神神社'''（みなかみじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]松代町豊栄の[[皆神山]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）に創建{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=皆神神社|publisher=信州松代観光協会|accessdate=2024-11-02}}。[[皆神山]]の山頂に位置し、'''熊野出速雄神社'''を本社とし、侍従神社や末社を併せて皆神神社と総称している[[1971年]],長野県史刊行会『長野県史：北信地方』p322。 本殿は[[1389年]]（[[康応]]元年）に再建され、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に長野県の文化財に指定された。 == 祭神 == * [[出速雄命]] * [[伊邪那岐尊]] * [[伊邪那美尊]] * [[速玉男命]] * [[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] == 境内社 == * 侍従神社 *: 祭神の侍従大は[[内山城]]主である内山満久の三男・三郎満顕のことで、内山氏滅亡の際に皆神山に入山し、大日寺和合院宥賢と称した。後に侍従天狗坊と名乗り、[[1556年]]（[[弘治]]2年）に侍従坊大天狗明王を祀った。 * 富士浅間神社 * 天満宮 * 弁天社 == 文化財 == ; 長野県指定文化財（県宝） * 熊野出速雄神社本殿 - [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に指定。 ː 長野市指定文化財 * 木造阿弥陀如来坐像・木造弥勒ぼ雑坐像 - [[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）[[1月20日]]に指定。 * 皆神山の[[クロサンショウウオ]]の産卵地 - [[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年）[[11月1日]]に指定。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[長野駅]]」より車で約30分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[長野インターチェンジ]]」より車で約30分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ 皆神神社] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ 皆神神社] - 信州松代観光協会 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みなかみしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:長野市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称= 皆神神社 |画像= {{画像募集中|cat=長野市}} |所在地= [[長野県]][[長野市]]松代町豊栄5454 |祭神= [[出速雄命]]
[[伊邪那岐尊]]
[[伊邪那美尊]]
[[速玉男命]]
[[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建= [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年） |本殿= |別名= 熊野出速雄神社 |例祭= [[10月10日]] |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''皆神神社'''（みなかみじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]]松代町豊栄の[[皆神山]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）に創建[[1971年]],長野県史刊行会『長野県史：北信地方』p322。[[皆神山]]の山頂に位置し、'''熊野出速雄神社'''を本社とし、侍従神社や末社を併せて皆神神社と総称している{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=皆神神社|publisher=信州松代観光協会|accessdate=2024-11-02}}。 本殿は[[1389年]]（[[康応]]元年）に再建され、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に長野県の文化財に指定された。 == 祭神 == * [[出速雄命]] * [[伊邪那岐尊]] * [[伊邪那美尊]] * [[速玉男命]] * [[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] == 境内社 == * 侍従神社 *: 祭神の侍従大は[[内山城]]主である内山満久の三男・三郎満顕のことで、内山氏滅亡の際に皆神山に入山し、大日寺和合院宥賢と称した。後に侍従天狗坊と名乗り、[[1556年]]（[[弘治]]2年）に侍従坊大天狗明王を祀った。 * 富士浅間神社 * 天満宮 * 弁天社 == 文化財 == ; 長野県指定文化財（県宝） * 熊野出速雄神社本殿 - [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に指定。 ; 長野市指定文化財 * 木造阿弥陀如来坐像・木造弥勒ぼ雑坐像 - [[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）[[1月20日]]に指定。 * 皆神山の[[クロサンショウウオ]]の産卵地 - [[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年）[[11月1日]]に指定。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[長野駅]]」より車で約30分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[長野インターチェンジ]]」より車で約30分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ 皆神神社] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ 皆神神社] - 信州松代観光協会 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みなかみしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:長野市の建築物]]

[[速玉男命]]
[[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建= [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年） |本殿= |別名= 熊野出速雄神社
侍従神社 |例祭= [[10月10日]] |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''皆神神社'''（みなかみじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]]松代町豊栄の[[皆神山]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）に創建[[1971年]],長野県史刊行会『長野県史：北信地方』p322。[[皆神山]]の山頂に位置し、'''熊野出速雄神社'''を本社とし、侍従神社や末社を含めて皆神神社と総称している{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=皆神神社|publisher=信州松代観光協会|accessdate=2024-11-02}}。 本殿は[[1389年]]（[[康応]]元年）に再建され、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に長野県の文化財に指定された。 == 祭神 == * [[出速雄命]] * [[伊邪那岐尊]] * [[伊邪那美尊]] * [[速玉男命]] * [[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] == 境内社 == * 侍従神社 *: 祭神の侍従大は[[内山城]]主である内山満久の三男・三郎満顕のことで、内山氏滅亡の際に皆神山に入山し、大日寺和合院宥賢と称した。後に侍従天狗坊と名乗り、[[1556年]]（[[弘治]]2年）に侍従坊大天狗明王を祀った。 * 富士浅間神社 * 天満宮 * 弁天社 == 文化財 == ; 長野県指定文化財（県宝） :* 熊野出速雄神社本殿 - [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に指定。 : ; 長野市指定文化財 :* 木造阿弥陀如来坐像・木造弥勒菩薩坐像 - [[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）[[1月20日]]に指定。 :* 皆神山の[[クロサンショウウオ]]の産卵地 - [[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年）[[11月1日]]に指定。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[長野駅]]」より車で約30分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[長野インターチェンジ]]」より車で約30分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ 皆神神社] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ 皆神神社] - 信州松代観光協会 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みなかみしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:長野市の建築物]]

{{Cite sources|date=November 5, 2024}} {{Shrine | name = Minakami Shrine | image = [[File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}} Matsushiro Town Toyosaka 5454 | deities = {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}}
{{ill|Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}
{{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} | shrine rank = {{ill|Former village shrine|ja|近代社格制度}} | founded = {{ill|718|ja|718年}} ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} 2) | main hall = | other names = Kumano Izuhaya Shrine
Jijū Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|718|ja|718年}} (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|ja|1389年}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994年8月15日}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556年}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * Approximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[ja:皆神神社]]

He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556年}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * Approximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Link complete pages]] [[ja:皆神神社]]

Jijū Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|718|ja|718年}} (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|ja|1389年}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994年8月15日}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556年}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * Approximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Link complete pages]] [[ja:皆神神社]]

He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556年}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * Approximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Minakami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:皆神神社]]

It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556年}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * Approximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Minakami Shrine-qq]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:ppp]]

It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|ja|1389年}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994年8月15日}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556年}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * Approximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:Link complete]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Minakami Shrine-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Minakami Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} | shrine rank = {{ill|Former village shrine|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} | founded = {{ill|718|ja|718年}} ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} 2) | main hall = | other names = Kumano Izuhaya Shrine
Jijū Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|718|ja|718年}} (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|ja|1389年}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994年8月15日}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556年}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Aqqroximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * Aqqroximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - {{出典の明記|date=2024年11月5日 (火) 06:33 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称= 皆神神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|300px]] |所在地= [[長野県]][[長野市]]松代町豊栄5454 |祭神= [[出速雄命]]
[[伊邪那岐尊]]
[[伊邪那美尊]]
[[速玉男命]]
[[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建= [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年） |本殿= |別名= 熊野出速雄神社
侍従神社 |例祭= [[10月10日]] |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''皆神神社'''（みなかみじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]]松代町豊栄の[[皆神山]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）に創建[[1971年]],長野県史刊行会『長野県史：北信地方』p322。[[皆神山]]の山頂に位置し、'''熊野出速雄神社'''を本社とし、侍従神社や末社を含めて皆神神社と総称している{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=皆神神社|publisher=信州松代観光協会|accessdate=2024-11-02}}。 [[1335年]]（[[建武]]2年）神官家を世襲して来た[[関屋氏]]が[[船山守護所]]の襲撃に加担したが撃退されて多くの戦死者を出して逃亡四散した。以後[[玉依姫神社]]の神主[[小河原氏]]が祭祀を担う。 本殿は[[1389年]]（[[康応]]元年）に再建され、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に長野県の文化財に指定された。 == 祭神 == * [[出速雄命]] * [[伊邪那岐尊]] * [[伊邪那美尊]] * [[速玉男命]] * [[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] == 境内社 == * 侍従神社 *: 祭神の侍従大は[[内山城]]主である内山満久の三男・三郎満顕のことで、内山氏滅亡の際に皆神山に入山し、大日寺和合院宥賢と称した。後に侍従天狗坊と名乗り、[[1556年]]（[[弘治]]2年）に侍従坊大天狗明王を祀った。 * 富士浅間神社 * 天満宮 * 弁天社 == 文化財 == ; 長野県指定文化財（県宝） :* 熊野出速雄神社本殿 - [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に指定。 : ; 長野市指定文化財 :* 木造阿弥陀如来坐像・木造弥勒菩薩坐像 - [[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）[[1月20日]]に指定。 :* 皆神山の[[クロサンショウウオ]]の産卵地 - [[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年）[[11月1日]]に指定。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[長野駅]]」より車で約30分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[長野インターチェンジ]]」より車で約30分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ 皆神神社] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ 皆神神社] - 信州松代観光協会 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みなかみしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:長野市の建築物]]

Jijū Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|718|ja|718年}} (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|ja|1389年}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994年8月15日}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556年}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - {{Cite sources|date=November 5, 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Minakami Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|300px]] | location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}} {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}} Matsushiro Town Toyosaka 5454 | deities = {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}}
{{ill|Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}}
{{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} | shrine rank = {{ill|Former village shrine|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} | founded = {{ill|718|ja|718年}} ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} 2) | main hall = | other names = Kumano Izuhaya Shrine
Jijū Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|718|ja|718年}} (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|ja|1389年}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994年8月15日}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556年}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. Jijū Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|718|ja|718th year }} (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|ja|1389th year }} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556th year }} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅}} (JR East) * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=draft}} | shrine rank = {{ill|Former village shrine|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} | founded = {{ill|718|ja|718th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老|12=draft}} 2) | main hall = | other names = Kumano Izuhaya Shrine
Jijū Shrine | festival = {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|12=draft}} | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine was founded in {{ill|718|ja|718th year|12=draft}} (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=draft}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|ja|1389th year|12=draft}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=draft}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|12=draft}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|ja|1556th year|12=draft}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|ja|富士浅間神社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|ja|天満宮|12=draft}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amida Nyorai|ja|阿弥陀如来|12=draft}} and {{ill|Maitreya|ja|弥勒菩薩|12=draft}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=draft}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|ja|長野駅|12=draft}} (JR East) * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|ja|長野インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|ja|上信越自動車道|12=draft}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Minakami Shrine]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Minakami Shrine]] [[ja:皆神神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Minakami Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2024年11月5日 (火) 06:33 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称= 皆神神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|300px]] |所在地= [[長野県]][[長野市]]松代町豊栄5454 |祭神= [[出速雄命]]
[[伊邪那岐尊]]
[[伊邪那美尊]]
[[速玉男命]]
[[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] |社格= [[近代社格制度|旧村社]] |創建= [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年） |本殿= |別名= 熊野出速雄神社
侍従神社 |例祭= [[10月10日]] |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''皆神神社'''（みなかみじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]]松代町豊栄の[[皆神山]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）に創建[[1971年]],長野県史刊行会『長野県史：北信地方』p322。[[皆神山]]の山頂に位置し、'''熊野出速雄神社'''を本社とし、侍従神社や末社を含めて皆神神社と総称している{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=皆神神社|publisher=信州松代観光協会|accessdate=2024-11-02}}。 [[1335年]]（[[建武]]2年）神官家を世襲して来た[[関屋氏]]が[[船山守護所]]の襲撃に加担したが撃退されて多くの戦死者を出して逃亡四散した。以後[[玉依姫神社]]の神主[[小河原氏]]が祭祀を担う。 本殿は[[1389年]]（[[康応]]元年）に再建され、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に長野県の文化財に指定された。 == 祭神 == * [[出速雄命]] * [[伊邪那岐尊]] * [[伊邪那美尊]] * [[速玉男命]] * [[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] == 境内社 == * 侍従神社 *: 祭神の侍従大は[[内山城]]主である内山満久の三男・三郎満顕のことで、内山氏滅亡の際に皆神山に入山し、大日寺和合院宥賢と称した。後に侍従天狗坊と名乗り、[[1556年]]（[[弘治]]2年）に侍従坊大天狗明王を祀った。 * 富士浅間神社 * 天満宮 * 弁天社 == 文化財 == ; 長野県指定文化財（県宝） :* 熊野出速雄神社本殿 - [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に指定。 : ; 長野市指定文化財 :* 木造阿弥陀如来坐像・木造弥勒菩薩坐像 - [[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）[[1月20日]]に指定。 :* 皆神山の[[クロサンショウウオ]]の産卵地 - [[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年）[[11月1日]]に指定。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[長野駅]]」より車で約30分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[長野インターチェンジ]]」より車で約30分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ 皆神神社] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ 皆神神社] - 信州松代観光協会 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みなかみしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:長野市の建築物]]

侍従神社 |例祭= [[10月10日]] |神事= |ラベル位置 = |地図 = |}} '''皆神神社'''（みなかみじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]]松代町豊栄の[[皆神山]]にある[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == [[718年]]（[[養老]]2年）に創建[[1971年]],長野県史刊行会『長野県史：北信地方』p322。[[皆神山]]の山頂に位置し、'''熊野出速雄神社'''を本社とし、侍従神社や末社を含めて皆神神社と総称している{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=皆神神社|publisher=信州松代観光協会|accessdate=2024-11-02}}。 [[1335年]]（[[建武]]2年）神官家を世襲して来た[[関屋氏]]が[[船山守護所]]の襲撃に加担したが撃退されて多くの戦死者を出して逃亡四散した。以後[[玉依姫神社]]の神主[[小河原氏]]が祭祀を担う。 本殿は[[1389年]]（[[康応]]元年）に再建され、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に長野県の文化財に指定された。 == 祭神 == * [[出速雄命]] * [[伊邪那岐尊]] * [[伊邪那美尊]] * [[速玉男命]] * [[事解之男神|予母都事解之男命]] == 境内社 == * 侍従神社 *: 祭神の侍従大は[[内山城]]主である内山満久の三男・三郎満顕のことで、内山氏滅亡の際に皆神山に入山し、大日寺和合院宥賢と称した。後に侍従天狗坊と名乗り、[[1556年]]（[[弘治]]2年）に侍従坊大天狗明王を祀った。 * 富士浅間神社 * 天満宮 * 弁天社 == 文化財 == ; 長野県指定文化財（県宝） :* 熊野出速雄神社本殿 - [[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）[[8月15日]]に指定。 : ; 長野市指定文化財 :* 木造阿弥陀如来坐像・木造弥勒菩薩坐像 - [[1993年]]（[[平成]]5年）[[1月20日]]に指定。 :* 皆神山の[[クロサンショウウオ]]の産卵地 - [[1967年]]（[[昭和]]42年）[[11月1日]]に指定。 == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]]「[[長野駅]]」より車で約30分。 * [[上信越自動車道]]「[[長野インターチェンジ]]」より車で約30分。 == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ 皆神神社] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ 皆神神社] - 信州松代観光協会 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みなかみしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:長野市の建築物]]

Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|qq=}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|qq=}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|qq=}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Minakami Shrine]] [[ja:皆神神社]]

{{Unreferenced|date=November 5, 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Minakami Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|300px]] | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]] Matsushiro Town Toyosaka 5454 | deities = {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|qq=}} | shrine Rank = [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] | founded = [[:en:718|718]] ([[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 2) | main hall = | other names = Kumano Izuhaya Shrine
Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|qq=}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|qq=}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|qq=}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Minakami Shrine]] [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]]

Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|qq=}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|qq=}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|qq=}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Nagano City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagano City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Minakami Shrine]] [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]]

Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 出早雄命}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * A11roximately 30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Minakami Shrine]] [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]]

He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately[[Category:11 to pp]] 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately[[Category:11 to pp]] 30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Minakami Shrine]] [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately[[Category:11 to pp]] 30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately[[Category:11 to pp]] 30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Minakami Shrine]] [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}}

He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}}

Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = | location = {{ill|Nagano|qid=Q128849}} }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}}

Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = | location = {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849}} }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = | location = {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849}} }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:皆神神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q131925931]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=November 5, 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Minakami Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg | location = [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]] Matsushiro Town Toyosaka 5454 | deities = {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}}
{{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
{{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayatama no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}}
{{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrine Rank = [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] | founded = [[:en:718|718]] ([[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 2) | main hall = | other names = Kumano Izuhaya Shrine
Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = | location = {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849}} }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]
