Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}}

He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:皆神神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}}

He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:皆神神社]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = | location = {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849}} }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:皆神神社]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Jijū Shrine | festival = [[:en:October 10|October 10]] | rituals = | label position = | map = | location = {{ill|Nagano|WD=Q128849}} }} '''Minakami Shrine''' (皆神神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]. == History == The shrine was founded in [[:en:718|718]] (Yōrō 2){{Cite book|title=Nagano Prefecture History: Hokushin Region|publisher=Nagano Prefectural History Association|year=1971|page=322}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in [[:en:1389|1389]] (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in [[:en:1556|1556]] (Kōji 2). * [[:en:Fuji Sengen Shrine|Fuji Sengen Shrine]] * [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangū]] * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amida Nyorai]] and [[:en:Maitreya|Maitreya]] (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from [[:en:Nagano Station|Nagano Station]] (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the [[:en:Nagano Interchange|Nagano Interchange]] on the [[:en:Jōshin-etsu Expressway|Jōshin-etsu Expressway]] == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:皆神神社]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|en|1389|WD=Q6371|lt=1389}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|en|1556|WD=Q6590|lt=1556}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|en|Fuji Sengen Shrine|WD=Q11456094|lt=Fuji Sengen Shrine}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|WD=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai}} and {{ill|Maitreya|en|Maitreya|WD=Q193461|lt=Maitreya}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|WD=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station}} (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|en|Nagano Interchange|WD=Q11654109|lt=Nagano Interchange}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|WD=Q1059866|lt=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:皆神神社]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|en|1389|WD=Q6371|lt=1389|1=1389}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. It is situated at the summit of Mount Minakami and is referred to collectively as '''Minakami Shrine''', encompassing the main {{ill|Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhaya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}, Jijū Shrine, and associated sub-shrines{{Cite web|url=https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/|title=Minakami Shrine|publisher=Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association|accessdate=November 2, 2024}}. The main hall was rebuilt in {{ill|1389|en|1389|qid=Q6371|lt=1389|1=1389}} (Kōō 1) and designated as a cultural property of Nagano Prefecture on {{ill|August 15, 1994|ja|1994th year 8月15日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 15, 1994|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|lt=Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja|出速雄命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izuhaya no Mikoto|ja_comment=|1=Izuhayao-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=出早雄命|}} * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} * {{ill|Kumano Gongen|lt=Kumano Gongen|lt=Hayatama no Mikoto|ja|速玉男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熊野権現}} * {{ill|Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja|予母都事解之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yomotsu Wakesu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Sub-Shrines == * '''Jijū Shrine''' ** Dedicated to Jijū Dai, the third son of Uchiyama Mitsuhisa, lord of {{ill|Uchiyama Castle|ja|内山城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchiyama Castle|qq=}}. Following the fall of the Uchiyama clan, he retreated to Mount Minakami, adopting the title Dainichi-ji Wago-in Yūken. He later became known as Jijū Tengu-bō and enshrined Jijūbō Daitengu Myōō in {{ill|1556|en|1556|qid=Q6590|lt=1556|1=1556}} (Kōji 2). * {{ill|Fuji Sengen Shrine|en|Fuji Sengen Shrine|qid=Q11456094|lt=Fuji Sengen Shrine|1=Fuji Sengen Shrine}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū|1=Tenmangū}} * {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁天社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} == Cultural Properties == ; Prefectural Cultural Properties (Nagano Prefecture Treasures) * The main hall of Kumano Izuhaya Shrine (designated on August 15, 1994) ; Municipal Cultural Properties (Nagano City) * Wooden seated statues of {{ill|Amitābha|en|Amitābha|qid=Q236242|lt=Amida Nyorai|1=Amitābha}} and {{ill|Maitreya|en|Maitreya|qid=Q193461|lt=Maitreya|1=Maitreya}} (designated on January 20, 1993) * {{ill|Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山のクロサンショウウオの産卵地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Breeding site for Black Salamanders on Mount Minakami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (designated on November 1, 1967) == Access == * Approximately30 minutes by car from {{ill|Nagano Station|en|Nagano Station|qid=Q374916|lt=Nagano Station|1=Nagano Station}} (JR East) * Approximately30 minutes by car from the {{ill|Nagano Interchange|en|Nagano Interchange|qid=Q11654109|lt=Nagano Interchange|1=Nagano Interchange}} on the {{ill|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|en|Jōshin-etsu Expressway|qid=Q1059866|lt=Jōshin-etsu Expressway|1=Jōshin-etsu Expressway}} == References == == External Links == * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Official Website of Minakami Shrine] * [https://www.matsushiro-kankou.com/spot/spot-049/ Minakami Shrine] - Shinshu Matsushiro Tourism Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|皆神神社|version=104160653|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:皆神神社]] {{wikidata link|Q131925931}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
